StatusChannelNoTvgId: This channel has no tvg-id — the guide can't match it.
StatusNoGuideRowForChannel: No guide row for {0} — try Refresh Guide…
StatusNoChannelsYet: No channels yet — click “+ Add Playlist”.
StatusPlaylistLoadingWait: A playlist is still loading — please wait…
StatusLayoutLimitReached: Layout limit reached ({0}) — delete one first.
StatusAddPlaylistFailedDb: Add playlist failed: could not save to the database
StatusGuideUrlSaved: Guide URL saved — run Settings ▸ Refresh Guide to fetch it
StatusScheduleOwnsPane: A scheduled recording owns this pane — manage it in Scheduled Recordings.
StatusPlayChannelFirst: Play a channel first, then Record.
StatusAiringCancelledRule: Airing cancelled — it came from a series rule, so it stays listed as Cancelled. Delete the rule to stop the series.
StatusNoCategories: No categories to filter — this library has no group titles.
StatusWakeToRecordOff: Wake-to-record off — recordings need the app running.
StatusWakeWillWake: This PC will wake for scheduled recordings.
StatusWakeTaskStarted: Wake task started — the scheduled-wake launch path is running now.
StatusWakeTaskFailed: Could not start the wake task. Check the log; is one queued?
DialogDeletePlaylistBody:
Delete playlist "{0}"?

This removes its channels from RabbitEars.
PlaylistDbSaveProblem: Problem:  the playlist could not be saved to the database.
TooltipBufferConsumption:
Consumption: %.2f Mb/s
Buffer (latency): %.1f s
Recent loss: %d%s
TooltipMeterFrames: Frame rate (flares red on dropped frames)
RefreshGuideNoSourceBody:
None of your enabled playlists carry an XMLTV guide URL (x-tvg-url in the #EXTM3U header).

Add a playlist that includes one, then try again.
LoadingBuildingGuide: Building the guide from stored programmes…
GuideNoGuideBody:
No stored programmes match a channel in your playlists.

Either run Settings ▸ Refresh Guide… first, or the guide's channel IDs don't match your playlist — point it at a guide whose tvg-ids line up (right-click a playlist ▸ Set Guide URL…).
GuideNoMatchBody:
Couldn't find a channel for "{0}" in your playlist.

The guide matches programmes to channels by tvg-id — this programme's channel ID has no match. Point the playlist at a guide whose IDs match it (right-click the playlist ▸ Set Guide URL…).
SetGuideUrlPrompt: XMLTV guide URL (.xml or .xml.gz; blank to clear):
SetGuideUrlSavedPrompt:
Guide URL saved.

Download the guide now?
ViewSwitchStopRecordingConfirm: Switching the view stops %d background recording(s) (the file will be finalized). Continue?
ScheduleChannelNotFoundBody: Couldn't match "{0}" to a playable channel in your library (its tvg-id isn't in an enabled playlist).
ScheduleCouldNotBody: The recording could not be saved.
RuleDeleteConfirm:
Delete the rule for "{0}"?

Upcoming recordings it queued will be removed. Recordings that already ran are kept.
RulesEmptyHint: Rules are created from the TV Guide: click a programme, then “Record series”. Every future airing is queued automatically.
RecordSeriesChannelNotFoundBody: Couldn't match "{0}" to a playable channel in your library.
RecordSeriesAlreadyBody:
A rule for "{0}" on {1} already exists.
See Settings ▸ Recording Rules….
RecordSeriesCouldNotSaveBody: The recording rule could not be stored.
RecordSeriesEveryAiringHeading: Recording every airing of this show
MetersHeading: Enable each meter, pick its look, and set its colours.
MeterDataFlowNote: The buffering tank shown at the far right of the transport bar. You can also hide it by right-clicking the meter itself.
AboutVersionLine:
A simple IPTV viewer for Windows.
Version {0}  ·  {1}
AboutLibVlcCredit:
Plays media with libVLC (LGPL-2.1)
© VideoLAN and the VLC contributors.
AboutEducationalDisclaimer: Rabbit Ears is provided only for educational purposes, and does not represent supporting any illegal activity that you do with it. We don't know, we don't care.
TermsBodyText:
Please read these terms before using RabbitEars. By choosing “I Accept” you agree to them. If you do not agree, choose “Decline” and the application will close.

1.  Educational purpose.  RabbitEars is a media player provided for educational and personal use only. It is offered “as is”, without warranty of any kind, and you use it entirely at your own risk.

2.  No content is included.  RabbitEars ships with no channels, playlists, or media of any kind. It plays only the playlists that you choose to add. You are solely responsible for obtaining your playlists from lawful sources and for ensuring that your use complies with all applicable laws and the rights of content owners in your jurisdiction.

3.  No endorsement.  The authors of RabbitEars do not provide, host, recommend, or endorse any stream or content, and have no knowledge of or control over what you choose to play. As the project puts it: we don’t know, and we don’t care.

4.  Your responsibility.  Any illegal activity carried out with this software is yours alone and is not supported, encouraged, or condoned by the authors.

5.  Open source.  RabbitEars plays media using libVLC, © VideoLAN and the VLC contributors, under the GNU LGPL v2.1.

By clicking “I Accept”, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to these terms.
SplashBendingLeftEar: Bending the left ear to the right…
SplashFindingArchCookies: Finding where Arch keeps his cookies…
CategoriesPrompt: Show channels only from the checked categories:
HintEmptyPip:
Picture-in-Picture
Right-click a channel ▸ Play in PIP
ExportFavouritesNoneBody: Star some channels first (the ★ column in the channel list, or right-click a TV-Guide row), then export.
CategoriesNoneBody:
The channels in your library have no group titles, so there are no categories to include or exclude.

Add a playlist whose #EXTINF lines carry group-title tags to use this filter.
AboutMacCredits:
A simple IPTV viewer for macOS.

Plays media with libVLC (LGPL-2.1)
© VideoLAN and the VLC contributors.

RabbitEars is provided only for educational purposes, and does not represent supporting any illegal activity that you do with it. We don't know, we don't care.
MacMainWindowDbUnavailableRestart: Database unavailable — check disk permissions and restart.
MacMainWindowReadyLoadHint: Ready — load an M3U URL or open a file to begin.
MacMainWindowAllPlaylistsDisabledManage: All playlists are disabled — enable one in Settings ▸ Manage Playlists….
MacMainWindowAllPlaylistsDisabledSee: All playlists are disabled — enable one to see channels.
MacMainWindowLastPlayedResume: Last played: {0} — double-click or press Return to resume.
MacMainWindowImportDbSaveBody:
Source: {0}

The playlist could not be saved to the database.
MacMainWindowImportNoChannelsBody:
Source: {0}

The playlist contained no channels.
MacMainWindowRecordingStartFailed: Couldn't start recording (the stream may cap concurrent connections).
MacMainWindowScheduleNoMatchBody: “{0}” has no matching channel in your playlists, so there's nothing to record.
MacMainWindowScheduledWakeCaveat: RabbitEars will record this while it is running and your Mac is awake. It can’t wake a sleeping Mac — for an unattended recording, keep the Mac on with the lid open (Settings ▸ Recording ▸ Recordings lists your queue).
MacMainWindowSplitViewStatus: Split view (2×2) — click a pane to make it active, then pick a channel.
MacMainWindowPipStatus: Picture-in-Picture — drag the inset to move it; click it to make it active.
MacMainWindowSaveLayoutPrompt: Name this layout (view mode, each pane's channel, and the PiP inset).
MacMainWindowLayoutLimitBody: You already have {0} saved layouts — delete one before saving another.
MacMainWindowLayoutMissingChannelsSuffix: ({0} channels no longer in your playlists)
MacMainWindowDeleteLayoutBody: This removes the saved layout. Your channels and playlists are untouched.
MacMainWindowNoGuideSourceBody:
None of your enabled playlists carry an XMLTV guide URL (the x-tvg-url in the #EXTM3U header).

Add a playlist that includes one, or set one per playlist in Manage Playlists ▸ 📅 Set Guide URL, then try again.
MacMainWindowGuideNoMatchBody:
Couldn't find a channel for “{0}” in your playlists.

The guide matches programmes to channels by tvg-id; this programme's ID has no match. Point the playlist at a guide whose IDs line up (Manage Playlists ▸ 📅 Set Guide URL).
MacMainWindowNoTvgIdBody:
“{0}” has no tvg-id, so it can't be matched to a guide row.

The TV Guide keys on tvg-id; channels without one never appear.
MacMainWindowNotInGuideYetBody:
“{0}” isn't in the guide yet.

No stored programmes match it — run Refresh Guide, or its tvg-id doesn't line up with the guide's IDs.
MacMainWindowExportNoFavesBody: Mark some channels with ★ first, then export.
MacMainWindowFavesUnmatchedSuffix: {0} entries in the file had no match in your playlists (import marks existing channels; it doesn't add new ones).
MacMainWindowChannelNumberPrompt: Enter a number, or leave blank to clear it.
MacRecordingsWakeHint: RabbitEars records only while it is running and your Mac is awake. It can’t wake a sleeping Mac to record — keep the Mac on (lid open, plugged in) for scheduled recordings.
MacRecordingsAiringQueuedByRuleHeading: This airing was queued by a series rule
MacRecordingsAiringQueuedByRuleBody: It’s cancelled and won’t record. The entry stays in the list because removing it now would let its series rule re-queue the same airing; you can remove it once the airing’s scheduled time has passed.
MacRecordingsDeleteRuleConfirmBody: Its still-pending queued recordings are removed too. Recordings that already ran are kept.
MacMetersDialogSubheading: Show, style, colour, and tune each meter — the preview updates live.
MacPlaylistsHeaderHint: Turn a playlist off to hide its channels from the grid and filters, or delete it entirely.
MacPlaylistsEmptyHint: No playlists yet — close this window, then use “＋ Add Playlist”.
MacPlaylistsToggleTooltip: Show or hide this playlist’s channels
MacPlaylistsDeleteConfirmBody: This permanently removes the playlist and its {0} channel(s) from RabbitEars, including any favourites and custom channel numbers you set on them. This cannot be undone.
MacPlaylistsRenameBody: This changes only the display name; the channels and source are untouched.
MacPlaylistsGuideUrlBody: XMLTV guide URL (.xml or .xml.gz). Leave blank to clear it. Then use “Refresh Guide” (the View menu or the ⚙ menu) to download it.
MacTvGuideNoGuideBody:
No stored programmes match a channel in your playlists.

Run Refresh Guide first — or the guide's channel IDs don't line up with your playlist. Point it at a guide whose tvg-ids match (Manage Playlists ▸ 📅 Set Guide URL).
MacEpgGuideViewNoGuideLoaded: No guide loaded — run Refresh Guide first.
MacMeterViewSpectrumUnavailableTooltip:
RabbitEars can’t read its own audio output, so the spectrum can’t be drawn.

Allow audio recording for RabbitEars in System Settings ▸ Privacy & Security, then relaunch the app.
